headline: Latest 'bad look' at Super Micro Computer batters stock, investor says
summary: STORY: Super Micro shares sank more than 30% on Friday after U.S. prosecutors charged three people linked with the company, including its co-founder, with helping smuggle billions of dollars worth of AI technology to China.U.S. prosecutors did not name Super Micro - a major AI server builder using Nvidia's chips - in the complaint. The company confirmed it was not named as a defendant in the case, and said it had cooperated with investigators.The share drop could erase more than $5 billion from Super Micro's $18.49 billion market value, if they hold."This is one of many negative headlines that have involved the company in the last couple of years," Orczyk Sissel said. "In 2024, Ernst and Young resigned as their auditor due to irregularities they saw, that they were uncomfortable continuing.... In 2020 they were fined for accounting violations. It's just one thing after another.""If what was happening with Super Micro right now was an isolated incident," she added, "it would not have impacted the stock as much as it has."
